title: Intensely Citrusy Filipino Jackfruit Stew
description: A comforting and citrusy Filipino dish featuring saffron and jackfruit, perfect for a chilly evening. This vegan-friendly stew is cooked to perfection in an earthenware bowl.
instructions: In a large earthenware bowl, heat the saffron threads over low heat for 1 minute.
Add the chopped onion, minced garlic, and grated ginger to the bowl and cook until the onion is translucent.
Add the jackfruit, lemon juice, lime juice, vegetable broth, salt, and black pepper to the bowl and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to medium-low and simmer for 20 minutes.
Stir the stew occasionally to prevent burning and to distribute the heat evenly.
After 20 minutes, reduce the heat to low and simmer for an additional 10 minutes, or until the stew has thickened slightly.
Remove the bowl from the heat and let the stew cool for a few minutes before serving.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
